messages.content: In V-CIP, what are the technical security requirements are explicitly specified for the infrastructure/application?

## Explicit technical security requirements for V-CIP infrastructure / application

The bank opting to undertake V-CIP “shall adhere to the following minimum standards” for “V-CIP Infrastructure” and related technical controls. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 1) Cyber / IT governance, hosting, network domain, outsourcing and cloud controls
- The bank “shall have complied with the RBI guidelines on minimum baseline cyber security and resilience framework for banks, as updated from time to time as well as other general guidelines on IT risks.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank “shall house the technology infrastructure in its own premises” and “the V-CIP connection and interaction shall necessarily originate from its own secured network domain.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- “Any technology related outsourcing for the process shall comply with relevant RBI guidelines.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- Where a cloud deployment model is used, the bank must ensure:
  - “ownership of data in such model rests with the bank only” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
  - “all the data including video recording is transferred to the bank’s exclusively owned / leased server(s) including cloud server, if any, immediately after the V-CIP process is completed” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
  - the “cloud service provider or third-party technology provider assisting the V-CIP shall retain no data.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 2) Encryption and consent integrity
- The bank “shall ensure end-to-end encryption of data between customer device and the hosting point of the V-CIP application, as per appropriate encryption standards.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank “shall record the customer consent in an auditable and alteration-proof manner.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 3) Geo/IP restriction controls
- The “V-CIP infrastructure / application shall be capable of preventing connection from IP addresses outside India or from spoofed IP addresses.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 4) Video-record security attributes and evidentiary quality
- “The video recordings shall contain the live GPS co-ordinates (geo-tagging) of the customer undertaking the V-CIP and date and time stamp.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- “The quality of the live video in the V-CIP shall be adequate to allow identification of the customer beyond doubt.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 5) Liveness/spoof detection, face matching and (optional) AI robustness
- The V-CIP application “shall have components with face liveness / spoof detection as well as face matching technology with high degree of accuracy” (while noting that “the ultimate responsibility of any customer identification rests with the bank”). [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- “The bank may use appropriate artificial intelligence (AI) technology to ensure that the V-CIP is robust.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 6) Security maintenance, event reporting, and mandated security testing
- The bank “shall regularly update the technology infrastructure including application software as well as workflows” based on experience of “detected / attempted / ‘near-miss’ cases of forged identity”. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank “shall report any detected case of forged identity through V-CIP as a cyber event under extant regulatory guidelines.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank “shall subject the V-CIP infrastructure to necessary tests such as Vulnerability Assessment, Penetration testing and a Security Audit to ensure its robustness and end-to-end encryption capabilities.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
  - “The bank shall mitigate any critical gap reported under this process before rolling out its implementation.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
  - “The empanelled auditors of Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) shall conduct such tests.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
  - Tests “shall also be carried out periodically in conformance to internal / regulatory guidelines.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank “shall subject the V-CIP application software and relevant APIs / webservices to appropriate testing of functional, performance, and maintenance strength before being used in live environment.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
  - The bank “shall roll out the application only after closure of any critical gap found during such tests.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
  - Such tests “shall also be carried out periodically in conformity with internal / regulatory guidelines.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 7) Technical records/data management controls (storage and audit trail)
- The bank “shall store the entire data and recordings of V-CIP in a system / systems located in India.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]
- The bank “shall ensure that the video recording is stored in a safe and secure manner and bears the date and time stamp that affords easy historical data search.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]
- The bank “shall preserve the activity log along with the credentials of the official performing the V-CIP.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]

### (Contextual definition relevant to security characteristics)
- V-CIP is defined as involving a “seamless, secure, live, informed-consent based audio-visual interaction… through independent verification and by maintaining an audit trail of the process.” [C. Definitions, p.13]

## What is not explicitly specified in the provided sections
- The provided extracts do **not** specify named encryption algorithms/bit-lengths or detailed cryptographic standards beyond “as per appropriate encryption standards.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The provided extracts do **not** specify numeric/video resolution or bandwidth parameters, only that quality must be “adequate to allow identification… beyond doubt.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
In V-CIP, what are the technical security requirements are explicitly specified for the infrastructure/application?

### V-CIP technical security requirements for the infrastructure / application (explicitly specified)

#### 1) Baseline cyber / IT risk compliance; hosting, network origination, outsourcing, cloud controls
- The bank **“shall have complied with the RBI guidelines on minimum baseline cyber security and resilience framework for banks… as well as other general guidelines on IT risks.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank **“shall house the technology infrastructure in its own premises”** and the **“V-CIP connection and interaction shall necessarily originate from its own secured network domain.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **Outsourcing controls:** **“Any technology related outsourcing for the process shall comply with relevant RBI guidelines.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **Cloud deployment controls:** where cloud is used, the bank must ensure **“ownership of data… rests with the bank only”** and **“all the data including video recording is transferred to the bank’s exclusively owned / leased server(s) including cloud server, if any, immediately after the V-CIP process is completed”**; and **“the cloud service provider or third-party technology provider assisting the V-CIP shall retain no data.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

#### 2) Encryption and consent capture
- The bank must ensure **“end-to-end encryption of data between customer device and the hosting point of the V-CIP application, as per appropriate encryption standards.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank must **“record the customer consent in an auditable and alteration-proof manner.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

#### 3) Network access / geofencing-type security
- The **“V-CIP infrastructure / application shall be capable of preventing connection from IP addresses outside India or from spoofed IP addresses.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

#### 4) Recording integrity attributes (geo-tagging; timestamp) and video quality
- Video recordings **“shall contain the live GPS co-ordinates (geo-tagging) of the customer… and date and time stamp.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **“The quality of the live video in the V-CIP shall be adequate to allow identification of the customer beyond doubt.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

#### 5) Anti-spoofing, liveness, and face matching technology
- The application **“shall have components with face liveness / spoof detection as well as face matching technology with high degree of accuracy.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, pp.31-32]
- RBI clarifies: **“Making specific facial gestures, like blinking of eyes, smiling, frowning, etc. is not mandatory for liveness check.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, pp.31-32]
- The bank **“may use appropriate artificial intelligence (AI) technology to ensure that the V-CIP is robust.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

#### 6) Patch / update expectations and cyber-event reporting (for forged identity cases)
- The bank must **“regularly update the technology infrastructure including application software as well as workflows”** based on detected/attempted/near-miss forged identity cases. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank must **“report any detected case of forged identity through V-CIP as a cyber event under extant regulatory guidelines.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

#### 7) Security testing requirements (VAPT; security audit; CERT-In empanelled auditors; periodic testing)
- The bank must test the V-CIP infrastructure via **“Vulnerability Assessment, Penetration testing and a Security Audit to ensure its robustness and end-to-end encryption capabilities.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank must **“mitigate any critical gap reported under this process before rolling out its implementation.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- **Who conducts tests:** **“The empanelled auditors of Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) shall conduct such tests.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- **Periodicity:** **“Such tests shall also be carried out periodically in conformance to internal / regulatory guidelines.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

#### 8) Application/API testing requirements (functional, performance, maintenance; close critical gaps; periodic)
- The bank must test **“the V-CIP application software and relevant APIs / webservices”** for **“functional, performance, and maintenance strength before being used in live environment.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank must **“roll out the application only after closure of any critical gap found during such tests.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- **Periodicity:** **“Such tests shall also be carried out periodically in conformity with internal / regulatory guidelines.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

#### 9) Data localization, secure storage, and logging (records/data management)
- The bank must **“store the entire data and recordings of V-CIP in a system / systems located in India.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]
- The bank must ensure video is stored **“in a safe and secure manner”** and **“bears the date and time stamp that affords easy historical data search.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]
- The bank must **“preserve the activity log along with the credentials of the official performing the V-CIP.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]

#### 10) “Secure” and audit-trail characteristics (definition-level requirements for V-CIP)
- V-CIP is defined as involving a **“seamless, secure, live, informed- consent based audio-visual interaction… through independent verification and by maintaining an audit trail of the process.”** [C. Definitions, p.13]

### What is not explicitly specified in the provided text
- The provided sections do **not** specify particular cryptographic algorithms/key lengths, exact “appropriate encryption standards,” detailed secure coding standards, device hardening requirements, or specific SIEM/log-retention durations for V-CIP beyond the requirements quoted above. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, pp.31-32; A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]

The V-CIP technical security requirements explicitly specified for the V-CIP infrastructure/application (minimum standards) are as follows:

## 1) Cybersecurity baseline and hosting / network domain controls
- The bank must have “complied with the RBI guidelines on minimum baseline cyber security and resilience framework for banks… as well as other general guidelines on IT risks.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank must “house the technology infrastructure in its own premises” and the “V-CIP connection and interaction shall necessarily originate from its own secured network domain.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

## 2) Outsourcing and cloud security / data ownership controls
- “Any technology related outsourcing for the process shall comply with relevant RBI guidelines.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- If a cloud deployment model is used, the bank must ensure:
  - “ownership of data in such model rests with the bank only”; and [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
  - “all the data including video recording is transferred to the bank’s exclusively owned / leased server(s) including cloud server, if any, immediately after the V-CIP process is completed”; and [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
  - “the cloud service provider or third-party technology provider assisting the V-CIP shall retain no data.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

## 3) Encryption and consent capture requirements
- The bank must “ensure end-to-end encryption of data between customer device and the hosting point of the V-CIP application, as per appropriate encryption standards.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank must “record the customer consent in an auditable and alteration-proof manner.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

## 4) IP / geo-fencing and anti-spoofing controls
- The “V-CIP infrastructure / application shall be capable of preventing connection from IP addresses outside India or from spoofed IP addresses.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

## 5) Video security metadata and quality requirements
- Video recordings must contain “the live GPS co-ordinates (geo-tagging) of the customer… and date and time stamp.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The “quality of the live video… shall be adequate to allow identification of the customer beyond doubt.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

## 6) Biometrics / anti-fraud technology requirements in the application
- The application must have “components with face liveness / spoof detection as well as face matching technology with high degree of accuracy” (while noting “ultimate responsibility of any customer identification rests with the bank”). [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank “may use appropriate artificial intelligence (AI) technology to ensure that the V-CIP is robust.” (permissive, not mandatory) [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

## 7) Patch/upgrade, cyber event reporting and resilience
- The bank must “regularly update the technology infrastructure including application software as well as workflows” based on detected/attempted/near-miss forged identity cases. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank must “report any detected case of forged identity through V-CIP as a cyber event under extant regulatory guidelines.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

## 8) Security testing / assurance requirements (including auditor qualification)
- The bank must subject V-CIP infrastructure to “necessary tests such as Vulnerability Assessment, Penetration testing and a Security Audit to ensure its robustness and end-to-end encryption capabilities,” and “mitigate any critical gap… before rolling out its implementation.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- “The empanelled auditors of Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) shall conduct such tests,” and these tests must also be conducted “periodically.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

## 9) Application/API testing requirements before production
- The bank must subject “V-CIP application software and relevant APIs / webservices” to “appropriate testing of functional, performance, and maintenance strength” before live use, and must “roll out the application only after closure of any critical gap.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

## 10) Data localisation and secure record storage (V-CIP records/data management)
- The bank must “store the entire data and recordings of V-CIP in a system / systems located in India.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]
- The bank must ensure video recording is stored “in a safe and secure manner” and “bears the date and time stamp that affords easy historical data search.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]
- The bank must “preserve the activity log along with the credentials of the official performing the V-CIP.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]

## What is NOT explicitly specified in the provided text
- The regulation requires end-to-end encryption “as per appropriate encryption standards” but does not specify particular cryptographic algorithms/protocol versions (e.g., TLS version/ciphers). [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- It requires “high degree of accuracy” for face matching but does not provide numeric accuracy/FAR/FRR thresholds. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- It mandates blocking “IP addresses outside India” / “spoofed IP addresses” but does not specify the exact technical method (e.g., specific IP intelligence or spoofing-detection technique). [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

## V-CIP technical security requirements explicitly specified for the infrastructure/application

### 1) Core infrastructure security, hosting, network origination, and outsourcing controls
- **Baseline cyber security compliance:** The bank **“shall have complied with the RBI guidelines on minimum baseline cyber security and resilience framework for banks… as well as other general guidelines on IT risks.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **On-premises + secured bank network origination:** The bank **“shall house the technology infrastructure in its own premises”** and **“the V-CIP connection and interaction shall necessarily originate from its own secured network domain.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **Outsourcing controls:** **“Any technology related outsourcing for the process shall comply with relevant RBI guidelines.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **Cloud model—data ownership, immediate transfer, and zero retention by third parties:** Where cloud is used, the bank must ensure **“ownership of data… rests with the bank only”** and that **“all the data including video recording is transferred to the bank’s exclusively owned / leased server(s) including cloud server, if any, immediately after the V-CIP process is completed”**; additionally, the **“cloud service provider or third-party technology provider assisting the V-CIP shall retain no data.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 2) Encryption, consent integrity, and access/channel security
- **End-to-end encryption:** The bank **“shall ensure end-to-end encryption of data between customer device and the hosting point of the V-CIP application, as per appropriate encryption standards.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **Consent capture with auditability and tamper resistance:** **“The bank shall record the customer consent in an auditable and alteration-proof manner.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **Prevent access from outside India / spoofed IPs:** The infrastructure/application **“shall be capable of preventing connection from IP addresses outside India or from spoofed IP addresses.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 3) Video/session security attributes (geo-tagging, timestamps, and quality)
- **Geo-tagging + timestamps embedded in recordings:** **“The video recordings shall contain the live GPS co-ordinates (geo-tagging) of the customer… and date and time stamp.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **Video quality sufficient for identification:** **“The quality of the live video in the V-CIP shall be adequate to allow identification of the customer beyond doubt.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 4) Identity assurance technology requirements in the application
- **Liveness/spoof detection + face matching with high accuracy:** The application **“shall have components with face liveness / spoof detection as well as face matching technology with high degree of accuracy”** (while noting that **“ultimate responsibility… rests with the bank.”**) [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **Facial gesture requirement not mandatory:** The Directions clarify: **“Making specific facial gestures, like blinking of eyes, smiling, frowning, etc. is not mandatory for liveness check.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **AI may be used to improve robustness (permitted):** **“The bank may use appropriate artificial intelligence (AI) technology to ensure that the V-CIP is robust.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 5) Ongoing security maintenance and incident reporting expectations
- **Regular updates based on forged identity attempts:** **“Based on experience of detected / attempted / ‘near-miss’ cases of forged identity, the bank shall regularly update the technology infrastructure including application software as well as workflows.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- **Reporting forged identity detected via V-CIP as a cyber event:** **“The bank shall report any detected case of forged identity through V-CIP as a cyber event under extant regulatory guidelines.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 6) Mandatory security assurance testing (infrastructure, application, APIs)
- **V-CIP infrastructure testing (VA/PT/Security Audit) + CERT-In empanelled auditors:** The bank **“shall subject the V-CIP infrastructure to necessary tests such as Vulnerability Assessment, Penetration testing and a Security Audit to ensure its robustness and end-to-end encryption capabilities.”** It must **“mitigate any critical gap… before rolling out”** and **“The empanelled auditors of Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) shall conduct such tests.”** These tests must also be carried out **“periodically”** as per internal/regulatory guidelines. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- **Application software and API/webservice testing before go-live + periodic re-testing:** The bank **“shall subject the V-CIP application software and relevant APIs / webservices to appropriate testing of functional, performance, and maintenance strength”** and **“roll out the application only after closure of any critical gap”**; such tests must also be carried out **“periodically.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 7) Data storage security/location obligations that directly affect V-CIP systems
- **Data/recordings must be stored in India, securely, with searchable timestamps:** The bank **“shall store the entire data and recordings of V-CIP in a system / systems located in India”** and ensure the recording is stored **“in a safe and secure manner and bears the date and time stamp that affords easy historical data search.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]
- **Audit trail expectation (definition-level security attribute):** V-CIP is defined as involving a **“seamless, secure, live… audio-visual interaction… through independent verification and by maintaining an audit trail of the process.”** [C. Definitions, p.13]

## What is not explicitly specified in the provided text
- The provided sections do **not** explicitly specify particular encryption algorithms/key sizes, specific device hardening controls, or named security standards beyond requiring **“appropriate encryption standards”** and compliance with RBI cyber security/resilience and IT risk guidelines (without reproducing those guidelines here). ## V-CIP — explicit technical security requirements for the infrastructure / application

The minimum standards explicitly specified for **V-CIP infrastructure / application** include the following:

### 1) Cybersecurity / hosting / network controls
- The bank **“shall have complied with the RBI guidelines on minimum baseline cyber security and resilience framework for banks… as well as other general guidelines on IT risks.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank shall **house the technology infrastructure in its own premises**, and the **V-CIP connection and interaction shall necessarily originate from its own secured network domain.** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **Any technology related outsourcing** for the V-CIP process **shall comply with relevant RBI guidelines.** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- Where a **cloud deployment model** is used, the bank shall ensure:
  - **“ownership of data… rests with the bank only”**; and
  - **“all the data including video recording is transferred to the bank’s exclusively owned / leased server(s) including cloud server, if any, immediately after the V-CIP process is completed”**; and
  - the cloud service provider / third-party technology provider **“shall retain no data.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 2) Encryption and consent integrity
- The bank shall ensure **“end-to-end encryption of data between customer device and the hosting point of the V-CIP application, as per appropriate encryption standards.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank shall **record the customer consent** in an **“auditable and alteration-proof manner.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 3) Geographic / IP-based access restrictions
- The V-CIP infrastructure / application shall be capable of **“preventing connection from IP addresses outside India or from spoofed IP addresses.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 4) Video integrity, geo-tagging, and quality
- The video recordings shall contain **“live GPS co-ordinates (geo-tagging) of the customer”** and **“date and time stamp.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The **quality of the live video** in V-CIP shall be **“adequate to allow identification of the customer beyond doubt.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 5) Anti-spoofing / biometric technology requirements
- The V-CIP application shall have components with **“face liveness / spoof detection as well as face matching technology with high degree of accuracy”** (while clarifying that the **ultimate responsibility of customer identification rests with the bank**). [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, pp.31-32]
- The bank may use **appropriate AI technology** to ensure V-CIP robustness. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 6) Continuous strengthening and cyber-event reporting
- Based on experience of forged identity cases, the bank shall **“regularly update the technology infrastructure including application software as well as workflows.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank shall **report any detected case of forged identity through V-CIP as a cyber event** under extant regulatory guidelines. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 7) Mandatory security testing and audit (infrastructure)
- The bank shall subject the V-CIP infrastructure to tests such as **“Vulnerability Assessment, Penetration testing and a Security Audit”** to ensure robustness and **end-to-end encryption capabilities.** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank shall **mitigate any critical gap** reported under this process **before rolling out** implementation. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- Such tests shall be conducted by **CERT-In empanelled auditors** and carried out **periodically** as per internal / regulatory guidelines. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 8) Pre-production and periodic testing (application software / APIs)
- The bank shall subject the **V-CIP application software and relevant APIs / webservices** to appropriate testing of **“functional, performance, and maintenance strength”** before live use. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank shall roll out the application **only after closure of any critical gap** found during such tests; and tests shall also be carried out **periodically** as per internal / regulatory guidelines. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 9) Data localisation and secure storage (records / data management)
- The bank shall **store the entire data and recordings of V-CIP in a system / systems located in India.** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]
- The bank shall ensure video recording is stored in a **“safe and secure manner”** and bears the **date and time stamp** enabling **“easy historical data search.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]
- The bank shall preserve the **activity log** along with the **credentials of the official** performing V-CIP. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]

### 10) V-CIP security characteristics embedded in the definition (context)
- V-CIP is defined as a process involving **“a seamless, secure, live, informed-consent based audio-visual interaction”** and requires the bank to maintain **“an audit trail of the process.”** [C. Definitions, p.13]

## What is not explicitly specified in the provided extracts
The retrieved sections do **not** explicitly specify (i) concrete encryption algorithms / key lengths (they refer to “appropriate encryption standards”), (ii) specific network security controls beyond the IP-address restriction, (iii) prescribed retention periods for V-CIP recordings/logs, or (iv) named technical standards for liveness/face-match accuracy benchmarks. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, pp.31-32; A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]

- The bank must **“record the customer consent in an auditable and alteration-proof manner.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

## 3) Geo-fencing / IP and anti-spoofing controls
- The V-CIP infrastructure/application must be capable of **“preventing connection from IP addresses outside India or from spoofed IP addresses.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

## 4) Video security metadata + quality for identification
- Video recordings must contain **“live GPS co-ordinates (geo-tagging) of the customer… and date and time stamp.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The **“quality of the live video… shall be adequate to allow identification of the customer beyond doubt.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

## 5) Biometric/AI controls for fraud resistance (application components)
- The application must include **“face liveness / spoof detection as well as face matching technology with high degree of accuracy”** (while noting that **“ultimate responsibility… rests with the bank.”**) [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank **“may use appropriate artificial intelligence (AI) technology to ensure that the V-CIP is robust.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

## 6) Security testing, audits, and remediation (pre-go-live and periodic)
- The bank must test the V-CIP infrastructure through **“Vulnerability Assessment, Penetration testing and a Security Audit to ensure its robustness and end-to-end encryption capabilities.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank must **“mitigate any critical gap… before rolling out its implementation.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- Such tests must be conducted by **“empanelled auditors of Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In)”** and also conducted **“periodically in conformance to internal / regulatory guidelines.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank must subject **“V-CIP application software and relevant APIs / webservices”** to **“appropriate testing of functional, performance, and maintenance strength”** before live use, roll out **“only after closure of any critical gap,”** and carry out such tests **“periodically”** per internal/regulatory guidelines. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

## 7) Continuous technology updates + cyber-event reporting for forged identity
- Based on detected/attempted/near-miss forged identity cases, the bank must **“regularly update the technology infrastructure including application software as well as workflows.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- Any detected case of forged identity through V-CIP must be reported **“as a cyber event under extant regulatory guidelines.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

## 8) Data localisation, secure storage, and audit logs (records/data management)
- The bank must **“store the entire data and recordings of V-CIP in a system / systems located in India.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]
- The bank must ensure the **“video recording is stored in a safe and secure manner”** and bears date/time stamp enabling **“easy historical data search.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]
- The bank must **“preserve the activity log along with the credentials of the official performing the V-CIP.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]

## Regulatory definition linkage (security-relevant characteristics)
- V-CIP is defined as involving a **“seamless, secure, live, informed-consent based audio-visual interaction”** and requires **“maintaining an audit trail of the process.”** [C. Definitions, p.13]

**Note on completeness:** The retrieved sections specify the above controls, but do **not** enumerate specific encryption algorithms/bit-strengths, specific IP-geo-fencing implementation methods, or detailed key management standards—only that encryption must be “as per appropriate encryption standards.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

## Explicit technical security requirements for V-CIP infrastructure / application (as specified)

- **Baseline cyber security compliance**: The bank **“shall have complied with the RBI guidelines on minimum baseline cyber security and resilience framework for banks… as well as other general guidelines on IT risks.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **On-premises infrastructure + secure network origination**: The bank **“shall house the technology infrastructure in its own premises”** and **“the V-CIP connection and interaction shall necessarily originate from its own secured network domain.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **Outsourcing controls**: **“Any technology related outsourcing for the process shall comply with relevant RBI guidelines.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **Cloud-specific data ownership and immediate transfer controls** (where cloud is used): The bank must ensure **“ownership of data… rests with the bank only”** and that **“all the data including video recording is transferred to the bank’s exclusively owned / leased server(s) including cloud server, if any, immediately after the V-CIP process is completed”**; further, **“the cloud service provider or third-party technology provider… shall retain no data.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 2) Encryption and consent integrity
- **End-to-end encryption**: The bank shall ensure **“end-to-end encryption of data between customer device and the hosting point of the V-CIP application, as per appropriate encryption standards.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- **Alteration-proof, auditable consent capture**: **“The bank shall record the customer consent in an auditable and alteration-proof manner.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 5) Liveness / spoof detection and face matching
- **Mandatory components**: The application **“shall have components with face liveness / spoof detection as well as face matching technology with high degree of accuracy”** (while **“ultimate responsibility… rests with the bank”).** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, pp.31-32]

- **AI usage permitted**: **“The bank may use appropriate artificial intelligence (AI) technology to ensure that the V-CIP is robust.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 6) Security maintenance, incident reporting, and hardening tests

CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

- **Mandatory VA/PT/Security Audit (CERT-In empanelled) + remediation before rollout**: The bank shall test the V-CIP infrastructure via **“Vulnerability Assessment, Penetration testing and a Security Audit”**; **“mitigate any critical gap… before rolling out”**; and **“The empanelled auditors of Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) shall conduct such tests”**, which must also be performed **“periodically”** as per internal/regulatory guidelines. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- **Application software + APIs/webservices testing (pre-production and periodic) + closure of critical gaps**: The bank shall test **“V-CIP application software and relevant APIs / webservices”** for **“functional, performance, and maintenance strength”**; **“roll out… only after closure of any critical gap”**; and test **“periodically.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 7) Data localization, secure storage, and logs (technical data management controls)
- **Data and recordings must be stored in India**: **“The bank shall store the entire data and recordings of V-CIP in a system / systems located in India.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]

title: Given a list of liabilities (incl. FCY interbank, offshore interbank, TREPS, NAB...

messages.content: Given a list of liabilities (incl. FCY interbank, offshore interbank, TREPS, NABARD/SIDBI refinance, call money), decide what counts inside IBL, what’s excluded, and compute the ratio. Would the bank qualify for 300% IBL and why?

## 1) Regulatory IBL cap and eligibility for 300%
- **Base cap:** “**IBL of a bank shall not exceed 200 per cent of its Net Worth as on March 31 of the previous year**.” A bank may, “**with the approval of its Board, fix a lower limit**” for its IBL. [N. Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities, p.22]
- **Higher cap (up to 300%):** A bank “**whose Capital to Risk-weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR) is at least 25 per cent more than the minimum CRAR (9 per cent), i.e., 11.25 per cent as on March 31 of the previous year is permitted to maintain a higher IBL of up to 300 per cent of its Net Worth**.” [N. Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities, p.22]

## 2) What counts *inside* IBL vs what is *excluded* (based on your listed liabilities)
### A. Items that are included in IBL (numerator)
The IBL limit “**shall include only fund-based IBL within India, including inter-bank liabilities in foreign currency to a bank operating within India**.” [N. Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities, p.22]
Accordingly, from your list:
- **FCY interbank liability (to a bank operating within India): Included** (explicitly included even if in foreign currency, provided the counterparty is “a bank operating within India”). [N. Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities, p.22]
- **Call money borrowings: Included in IBL, but also subject to a sub-limit** because “**The call money borrowing limit shall operate as a sub-limit within the above IBL limit**” (and the bank “shall be guided by” the separate call money market directions for that sub-limit). [N. Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities, p.22]

### B. Items excluded from IBL (numerator)
- **Offshore interbank (IBL outside India): Excluded if it is IBL outside India** because “**The IBL outside India shall be excluded**.” [N. Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities, p.22]
- **TREPS (Tri-Party Repo) collateralised borrowings: Excluded** because “**The IBL limits shall not include collateralized borrowings under Tri-Party Repo (TREPS)** …” [N. Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities, p.22]
- **NABARD/SIDBI (etc.) refinance: Excluded** because the IBL limits “**shall not include … refinance from NABARD, SIDBI, etc.**” [N. Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities, p.22]

## 3) How to compute the IBL ratio (and why your numeric ratio cannot be computed from the provided inputs)
### Step 1 — Compute Net Worth (denominator)
“**Net Worth** comprises of **Paid-up capital plus Free Reserves including Share Premium but excluding Revaluation Reserves, plus Investment Fluctuation Reserve, and credit balance in Profit & Loss Account, less** debit balance in Profit and Loss Account, Accumulated Losses, and Intangible Assets. **No general or specific provisions should be included** in computation of Net Worth.” [C. Definitions, pp.6-8]
Also, the IBL percentage test is against “**Net Worth as on March 31 of the previous year**.” [N. Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities, p.22]

### Step 2 — Compute Included IBL (numerator)
Included IBL = sum of **fund-based inter-bank liabilities within India**, including **inter-bank liabilities in foreign currency to a bank operating within India**, **plus call money borrowings** (since call money operates as a sub-limit *within* IBL). Exclude (i) IBL outside India, (ii) TREPS collateralised borrowings, and (iii) NABARD/SIDBI (etc.) refinance. [N. Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities, p.22]

### Step 3 — Ratio
**IBL ratio (%) = (Included IBL ÷ Net Worth) × 100**. The regulatory caps are 200% (default) or 300% (only if CRAR ≥ 11.25% as on March 31 of the previous year). [N. Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities, p.22]

### What is missing to compute your ratio numerically
To compute an actual number, you must provide:
- Amounts for each listed liability bucket (FCY interbank-in-India, offshore/outside-India interbank, TREPS borrowings, NABARD/SIDBI refinance, call money), and
- The bank’s **Net Worth as on March 31 of the previous year** (computed per the definition), and
- The bank’s **CRAR as on March 31 of the previous year** to assess 300% eligibility. [N. Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities, p.22; C. Definitions, pp.6-8]

## 4) Would the bank qualify for 300% IBL and why?
- The bank qualifies to “**maintain a higher IBL of up to 300 per cent of its Net Worth**” **only if** its “**CRAR … is … 11.25 per cent as on March 31 of the previous year**.” [N. Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities, p.22]
- If CRAR as on that date is **below 11.25%**, the applicable cap remains “**200 per cent of its Net Worth as on March 31 of the previous year**” (subject to any Board-approved lower internal limit). [N. Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities, p.22]

(Separately, the bank must also monitor concentration and compliance with these limits as part of liquidity monitoring and reporting expectations, including “adhering to limits under Inter-bank liability … and limit for call borrowings …”.) [L.2 Concentration of Funding, p.76]

title: In V-CIP, what exactly must be embedded in the video recordings?

messages.content: In V-CIP, what exactly must be embedded in the video recordings?

Under the RBI-prescribed minimum standards for V-CIP, the **video recordings must embed** the following:

- **Live GPS co-ordinates (geo-tagging) of the customer undertaking the V-CIP**, and a **date and time stamp**: “**The video recordings shall contain the live GPS co-ordinates (geo-tagging) of the customer undertaking the V-CIP and date and time stamp**.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.31), p.31]

Related recording requirements that apply alongside the above (not phrased as “embedded”, but directly governing the recording):

- The authorised official must **record audio and video** (i.e., the interaction must be recorded): “**The authorised official… shall record audio and video**…” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.33), p.33]
- The **quality of the live video** must be sufficient to identify the customer conclusively: “**The quality of the live video in the V-CIP shall be adequate to allow identification of the customer beyond doubt.**” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.31), p.31]
- For record management, the bank must ensure the **video recording bears the date and time stamp** to support searchability: “**the video recording is stored in a safe and secure manner and bears the date and time stamp that affords easy historical data search.**” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.34), p.34]

title: In Digital KYC OTP-signature, what is allowed if customer has no mobile number A...

messages.content: In Digital KYC OTP-signature, what is allowed if customer has no mobile number AND what is explicitly prohibited?

## Digital KYC OTP-as-signature: if the customer has **no own mobile number**, what is allowed?

Under the Digital KYC process, after the Customer Application Form (CAF) is completed, an OTP is sent and (on successful validation) is treated as the customer’s signature on the CAF [169MD (1).pdf | A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.28].

If the customer **does not have their own mobile number**, then:
- The bank **may use the mobile number of the customer’s “family / relatives / known persons”** for the OTP/signature purpose [169MD (1).pdf | A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.28].
- The bank must **“clearly mention it in the CAF”** that such family/relative/known person’s mobile number has been used [169MD (1).pdf | A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.28].

## What is explicitly prohibited?

Even when the customer does not have their own mobile number:
- **“In any case, the bank shall not use the mobile number of authorised officer registered with the bank for the customer signature.”** [169MD (1).pdf | A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.28]
- The bank must implement checks: **“The bank shall check that the mobile number used in customer signature is not the mobile number of the authorised officer.”** [169MD (1).pdf | A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.28]

## What is not specified in the provided text

The provided extract does **not** state what the bank should do if the customer has no mobile number **and** no family/relative/known person’s mobile number is available for OTP validation (i.e., an alternative method of capturing customer signature for Digital KYC is not described in the retrieved sections).

- The bank must preserve audit artifacts: **“The bank shall preserve the activity log along with the credentials of the official performing the V-CIP.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]

### Contextual definition reinforcing the security posture (as defined)
- V-CIP is defined as a process involving **“a seamless, secure, live, informed-consent based audio-visual interaction… through independent verification and by maintaining an audit trail of the process.”** [C. Definitions, p.13]

## What is not explicitly specified in the provided text
The extracted provisions do **not** explicitly specify (in the provided sections): exact cryptographic algorithms/key lengths, specific “appropriate encryption standards” by name, specific network security architecture patterns, or concrete log-retention periods for V-CIP artifacts (beyond requiring secure storage in India and preservation of activity logs). [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, pp.31-34]

messages.content: In V-CIP, what are the technical security requirements are explicitly specified for the infrastructure/application?

## Explicit technical security requirements for V-CIP infrastructure / application (as specified)

The minimum standards for **V-CIP Infrastructure** and related **application security** explicitly require the bank to implement the following controls:

### 1) Baseline cyber security compliance + secure hosting/network origin
- The bank **“shall have complied with the RBI guidelines on minimum baseline cyber security and resilience framework for banks… as well as other general guidelines on IT risks.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank **“shall house the technology infrastructure in its own premises”** and the **“V-CIP connection and interaction shall necessarily originate from its own secured network domain.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 2) Outsourcing / cloud security and data ownership / non-retention
- **Any technology related outsourcing** for V-CIP **“shall comply with relevant RBI guidelines.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- Where a **cloud deployment model** is used, the bank must ensure:
  - **“ownership of data in such model rests with the bank only”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
  - **“all the data including video recording is transferred to the bank’s exclusively owned / leased server(s) including cloud server, if any, immediately after the V-CIP process is completed”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
  - the **cloud service provider / third-party technology provider** assisting V-CIP **“shall retain no data.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 3) Encryption + consent capture integrity
- The bank must ensure **“end-to-end encryption of data between customer device and the hosting point of the V-CIP application, as per appropriate encryption standards.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank must **“record the customer consent in an auditable and alteration-proof manner.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 4) Network access controls (India-only / anti-spoofing requirement)
- The infrastructure/application must be capable of **“preventing connection from IP addresses outside India or from spoofed IP addresses.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 5) Security requirements for video session evidencing
- Video recordings must contain **“the live GPS co-ordinates (geo-tagging) of the customer… and date and time stamp.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank must ensure **“quality of the live video… [is] adequate to allow identification of the customer beyond doubt.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 6) Anti-spoofing / biometric robustness components
- The application must have **“components with face liveness / spoof detection as well as face matching technology with high degree of accuracy”** (while noting that **“ultimate responsibility of any customer identification rests with the bank.”**) [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank **“may use appropriate artificial intelligence (AI) technology to ensure that the V-CIP is robust.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 7) Continuous strengthening + incident reporting as cyber event
- Based on detected/attempted/near-miss forged identity cases, the bank **“shall regularly update the technology infrastructure including application software as well as workflows.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank **“shall report any detected case of forged identity through V-CIP as a cyber event under extant regulatory guidelines.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 8) Mandatory security testing/audit of V-CIP infrastructure (pre-rollout + periodic)
- The bank must subject the V-CIP infrastructure to **“necessary tests such as Vulnerability Assessment, Penetration testing and a Security Audit to ensure its robustness and end-to-end encryption capabilities.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank must **“mitigate any critical gap reported under this process before rolling out its implementation.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- **“The empanelled auditors of Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) shall conduct such tests.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- Testing must also be performed **“periodically in conformance to internal / regulatory guidelines.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 9) Mandatory testing of V-CIP application software + APIs/webservices (pre-live + periodic)
- The bank must subject **“the V-CIP application software and relevant APIs / webservices to appropriate testing of functional, performance, and maintenance strength before being used in live environment.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank must **“roll out the application only after closure of any critical gap found during such tests.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- Such tests must also be conducted **“periodically in conformity with internal / regulatory guidelines.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 10) Data localisation + secure storage + audit trail logs
- The bank must **“store the entire data and recordings of V-CIP in a system / systems located in India.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]
- The bank must ensure the video recording is **“stored in a safe and secure manner and bears the date and time stamp that affords easy historical data search.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]
- The bank must **“preserve the activity log along with the credentials of the official performing the V-CIP.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]

## What is not explicitly specified in the provided text
- The provided sections do **not** specify particular encryption algorithms/key lengths, specific secure coding standards, specific log retention periods, or specific security monitoring/SIEM controls for V-CIP—only the high-level requirements above (e.g., “end-to-end encryption… as per appropriate encryption standards”, testing/audit, India-only IP access, data localisation). [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, pp.31-32; A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.34]

- The V-CIP infrastructure / application must be capable of **“preventing connection from IP addresses outside India or from spoofed IP addresses.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 5) Video-record security metadata and quality requirements
- Video recordings must contain **“live GPS co-ordinates (geo-tagging)… and date and time stamp.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]
- The bank must ensure **“The quality of the live video… shall be adequate to allow identification of the customer beyond doubt.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.31]

### 6) Anti-spoofing / biometrics controls
- The application must include **“face liveness / spoof detection as well as face matching technology with high degree of accuracy”** (while noting that responsibility remains with the bank). [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, pp.31-32]
- The bank **“may use appropriate artificial intelligence (AI) technology to ensure that the V-CIP is robust.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 7) Ongoing security maintenance, event reporting, and periodic upgrades
- The bank must **“regularly update the technology infrastructure including application software as well as workflows”** based on detected/attempted/near-miss forged identity cases. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]
- The bank must **“report any detected case of forged identity through V-CIP as a cyber event under extant regulatory guidelines.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 8) Mandatory security testing / audits (VAPT and security audit) with conditions for rollout
- The bank must test V-CIP infrastructure via **“Vulnerability Assessment, Penetration testing and a Security Audit to ensure its robustness and end-to-end encryption capabilities.”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.32]

### 11) Security characteristics embedded in the definition of V-CIP
- V-CIP is defined as involving a **“seamless, secure, live, informed-consent based audio-visual interaction”** and requires **“maintaining an audit trail of the process.”** [C. Definitions, p.13]

title: Which limits are breached (if any), what immediate operational restrictions must...

messages.content: Which limits are breached (if any), what immediate operational restrictions must be applied, and by what deadline must the bank complete full identification (and what happens if it doesn’t)? Include how you’d handle the required customer declaration about not having OTP-based accounts elsewhere.

## 1) Limits to test for breach (and whether any are breached)
The retrieved sections do not provide your customer’s *actual* balances/credits/loan sanctions, so it is not possible to conclude whether a breach has occurred without those figures. What can be stated precisely is the regulatory limit-set you must test against for **accounts opened using Aadhaar OTP based e-KYC in non-face-to-face mode**:

### Deposit-side limits (OTP-based e-KYC, non-face-to-face)
- **Aggregate balance cap (all deposit accounts, taken together):** “**The aggregate balance of all the deposit accounts of the customer shall not exceed Rupees One Lakh.**” [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.29), p.29]
- **Aggregate annual credits cap (all deposit accounts, taken together):** “**The aggregate of all credits in a financial year, in all the deposit accounts taken together, shall not exceed Rupees Two Lakh.**” [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]

### Borrowal-side limits (OTP-based e-KYC, non-face-to-face)
- **Only term loans permitted:** “**As regards borrowal accounts, the bank shall sanction only term loans.**” [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]
- **Annual cap on term loans sanctioned:** “**The aggregate amount of term loans sanctioned shall not exceed ₹60,000 in a year.**” [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]

## 2) Immediate operational restrictions to apply
### A. If the aggregate balance exceeds ₹1,00,000
- The Direction requires an immediate stoppage of operations: “**In case the balance exceeds the threshold, the bank shall cease the account’s operation, until it completes the CDD** …” [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]
  - This is an immediate operational control: cease operations **until** completion of the specified CDD/identification route. [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]

### B. Controls that must be in place for OTP-based e-KYC accounts (ongoing operational restrictions/controls)
- **Customer consent for OTP authentication:** “**The Customer shall give specific consent for the authentication through OTP.**” [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.29), p.29]
- **Send transaction alerts/OTPs only to Aadhaar-registered mobile; have policy for mobile change:** as a “**risk-mitigating measure**,” the bank must ensure it “**sends transaction alerts, OTP, etc., only to the mobile number of the customer registered with Aadhaar**” and must have “**a Board-approved policy delineating a robust process of due diligence for dealing with requests for change of mobile number in such accounts.**” [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.29), p.29]
- **Monitoring and alerts for non-compliance/violation:** the bank must have “**strict monitoring procedures including systems to generate alerts in case of any non-compliance / violation**” to ensure compliance with the conditions. [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]

## 3) Deadline to complete full identification (CDD) and consequences if not completed
### Deadline
- The account cannot continue to operate beyond one year on the basis of OTP-based e-KYC alone: “**The bank shall not allow accounts, both deposit and borrowal, opened using OTP based e-KYC to operate for more than one year unless it carries out identification as per paragraph 23 or as per paragraphs 26 and 27 (V-CIP).**” [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]
- If the bank uses Aadhaar details under V-CIP for this purpose, it must follow the full process “**including fresh Aadhaar OTP authentication.**” [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]

### What happens if it doesn’t complete within one year
If the bank does not complete the required CDD/identification “**within a year**”:
- **Deposit accounts:** “**the bank shall close the same immediately**” [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]
- **Borrowal accounts:** “**the bank shall allow no further debits**” [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]

## 4) Handling the required customer declaration about not having OTP-based accounts elsewhere
For accounts opened using OTP based e-KYC in non-face-to-face mode:
- The bank “**shall obtain declaration from the customer to the effect that no other account has been opened nor will be opened using OTP based KYC in non-face-to-face mode with any other RE.**” [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]
- Additionally, when uploading KYC information to CKYCR, the bank must “**clearly indicate that such accounts are opened using OTP based e-KYC**” and that “**other REs shall not open accounts based on the KYC information of accounts opened with OTP based e-KYC procedure in non-face- to-face mode.**” [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]

### Practical operationalization (limited to what the text supports)
- Operationally, you must (i) collect the customer’s declaration with the specified wording/meaning, and (ii) ensure CKYCR upload is flagged exactly as required (i.e., clearly indicating OTP-based e-KYC, non-face-to-face), because both are stated as mandatory “shall” requirements. [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]

## Information missing to answer “which limits are breached” definitively
To determine breaches, you would need at least:
- Current **aggregate balance** across all deposit accounts opened using OTP-based e-KYC (to compare to ₹1,00,000 cap). [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.29), p.29]
- **Aggregate credits** in the current financial year across all deposit accounts (to compare to ₹2,00,000 cap). [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]
- For borrowal: confirmation that only **term loans** were sanctioned and the **aggregate sanctioned** in the year (to compare to ₹60,000 cap). [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]
- Account opening date (or date of OTP e-KYC onboarding) to measure the **one-year** CDD completion deadline. [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.30), p.30]

Identify all Beneficial Owners you must determine for X, and explain why each qualifies (ownership vs control vs “senior managing official” fallback). Also list whose CDD you must perform (customer, BOs, authorised signatories, POA holders, etc.) and what evidence you’d rely on.

{
  "answer_text": "## 1) Beneficial Owners (BOs) you must determine for “X” — depends on the legal form of X

Because the BO definition is legal-form specific, the BO set you must identify for X depends on whether X is a **company**, **partnership firm**, **unincorporated association/body of individuals**, or a **trust**. The RBI definition is always in terms of **natural person(s)** (even where ownership/control is exercised through other entities). [C. Definitions, p.5]

### A. If X is a **company**
You must determine as BO(s) the **natural person(s)** who (acting alone / together / through one or more juridical persons):
- **Ownership basis (threshold test):** have a “**controlling ownership interest**”, i.e., “**ownership of / entitlement to more than 10 percent of the shares or capital or profits of the company**”. This qualifies them as BOs on an **ownership** basis. [C. Definitions, p.5]
- **Control basis (control through other means):** even if they do not cross the ownership threshold, any natural person who “**exercises control through other means**” qualifies as a BO on a **control** basis. “**Control**” is defined to “**include the right to appoint the majority of the directors or to control the management or policy decisions including by virtue of their shareholding or management rights or shareholders agreements or voting agreements**.” [C. Definitions, p.5]

### B. If X is a **partnership firm**
You must determine as BO(s) the natural person(s) who (acting alone / together / through one or more juridical persons):
- **Ownership basis (threshold test):** have “**ownership of / entitlement to more than 10 percent of capital or profits of the partnership**.” [C. Definitions, p.5]
- **Control basis:** or who “**exercises control through other means**,” where “control” “**shall include the right to control the management or policy decision**.” [C. Definitions, p.5]

### C. If X is an **unincorporated association or body of individuals** (including societies)
You must determine as BO(s) the natural person(s) who (acting alone / together / through one or more juridical persons):
- **Ownership basis (threshold test):** have “**ownership of / entitlement to more than 15 percent of the property or capital or profits**” of the unincorporated association/body of individuals. [C. Definitions, p.6]
- **Senior managing official fallback (only if no BO identified under the threshold/control tests for (a)/(b)/(c)):** “**Where no natural person is identified under (a), (b) or (c) above, the beneficial owner is the relevant natural person who holds the position of senior managing official.**” This qualifies as the BO on a **fallback** basis. [C. Definitions, p.6]

Notes relevant to classification:
- “**body of individuals includes societies**.” [C. Definitions, p.6]
- For documentation purposes, “**Unregistered trusts / partnership firms shall be included under the term ‘unincorporated association’.**” [D. Identification of Beneficial Owner, p.39]

### D. If X is a **trust**
The BO identification for a trust is not a single threshold owner test. Instead, “**the identification of beneficial owner(s) shall include identification of**” all of the following categories (each qualifies because the regulation explicitly includes them in the BO set):
- **Author of the trust** [C. Definitions, p.6]
- **Trustee** [C. Definitions, p.6]
- **Beneficiaries with 10 percent or more interest in the trust** (threshold inclusion) [C. Definitions, p.6]
- **Any other natural person exercising ultimate effective control over the trust through a chain of control or ownership** (control inclusion) [C. Definitions, p.6]

## 2) BO identification & verification obligation (what you must do once you determine who the BOs are)

### A. When opening an account for a legal person
“For opening an account of a **Legal Person** who is not a natural person, the bank shall **identify the beneficial owner(s)** and shall undertake **all reasonable steps** … **to verify their identity**….” [D. Identification of Beneficial Owner, p.39]

### B. Exception for listed entities (and certain related entities)
Where “**the customer or the owner of the controlling interest**” is:
- “**an entity listed on a stock exchange in India**”, or
- “**an entity resident in jurisdictions notified by the Central Government and listed on stock exchanges in such jurisdictions**”, or
- “**a subsidiary of such listed entities**”,
then “**it is not necessary to identify and verify the identity of any shareholder or beneficial owner of such entities**.” [E. On-going Due Diligence, pp.40-44]

## 3) Whose CDD you must perform (customer, BOs, authorised signatories, POA holders, etc.)

### A. Individuals covered by the CDD procedure (explicit scope)
For CDD, the bank must obtain prescribed KYC inputs from an individual while:
- “**establishing an account-based relationship**” with the individual (i.e., where the customer is an individual), OR
- “**dealing with the individual who is a beneficial owner, authorised signatory or the power of attorney holder related to any legal entity**.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.25]

Accordingly, within the scope of the provided text, CDD is explicitly required for:
- the **individual customer** (where the customer is an individual) [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.25]
- each **individual beneficial owner** of a legal entity customer [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.25]
- each **individual authorised signatory** of a legal entity customer [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.25]
- each **individual power of attorney (POA) holder** transacting “related to any legal entity” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.25]

### B. Persons acting on behalf of certain juridical persons (authorised persons and attorney holders)
For opening an account of certain juridical persons (e.g., “**societies, universities and local bodies like village panchayats, etc.**”), or where someone “**purports to act on behalf of such juridical person or individual or trust**,” the bank must obtain and verify certified copies of:
- “**Document showing name of the person authorised to act on behalf of the entity**” [D. Identification of Beneficial Owner, p.39]
- “**Documents, as specified in paragraph 23, of the person holding an attorney to transact on its behalf**” [D. Identification of Beneficial Owner, p.39]
- “**Such documents as may be required by the bank to establish the legal existence of such an entity / juridical person**.” [D. Identification of Beneficial Owner, p.39]

### C. Trust/nominee/fiduciary arrangements — intermediaries and persons represented
“In cases of **trust / nominee or fiduciary accounts**, the bank determines whether the customer is acting on behalf of another person as trustee / nominee or any other intermediary.” In such cases, the bank shall obtain “**satisfactory evidence of the identity of the intermediaries and of the persons on whose behalf they are acting, as well as details of the nature of the trust or other arrangements in place**.” [E. On-going Due Diligence, pp.40-44]

## 4) Evidence you would rely on (CDD/KYC evidence for each relevant person)

### A. Evidence for any individual for whom CDD is required (customer / BO / authorised signatory / POA holder)
The CDD inputs the bank must obtain from the individual include:
- **Aadhaar number** in specified cases, including when they “**decide to submit their Aadhaar number voluntarily to a bank** …” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.25]
- **Proof of possession of Aadhaar number** where the bank can carry out offline verification [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.25]
- **Proof of possession of Aadhaar number (where offline verification cannot be carried out) or any OVD** (or “**equivalent e-document**”) containing identity and address details [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.25]
- **KYC Identifier** with explicit consent to download from CKYCR [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.25]
- **PAN** (or equivalent e-document) or **Form No. 60** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.25]
- Any other documents the bank may require, including regarding “**nature of business and financial status**” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.25]

### B. Verification / reliance pathway tied to what the individual submits
Depending on what is provided, the bank’s reliance/verification mechanism is explicitly specified:
- If Aadhaar number is submitted under the Aadhaar-number clause, the bank “**shall carry out authentication … using UIDAI’s e-KYC authentication facility**.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.25]
- If proof of possession of Aadhaar is submitted where offline verification can be carried out, the bank “**shall carry out offline verification**.” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.26]
- If an “**equivalent e-document of any OVD**” is submitted, the bank shall “**verify the digital signature**” as per IT Act and “**take a live photo**” as specified under paragraph 24. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.26]
- If any OVD / proof of possession of Aadhaar is submitted where offline verification cannot be carried out, the bank shall carry out “**verification through digital KYC**” as specified under paragraph 24. [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.26]
- If KYC Identifier is submitted, the bank shall “**retrieve the KYC records online from the CKYCR** ….” [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.26]

### C. Evidence for the legal entity/juridical person itself (legal existence + authority)
For relevant juridical persons, the bank relies on:
- document evidencing who is “**authorised to act on behalf of the entity**” [D. Identification of Beneficial Owner, p.39]
- the paragraph 23 CDD document set for the “**person holding an attorney to transact on its behalf**” [D. Identification of Beneficial Owner, p.39; A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.25]
- documents sufficient to “**establish the legal existence of such an entity / juridical person**.” [D. Identification of Beneficial Owner, p.39]

### D. Evidence in trust/nominee/fiduciary arrangements
Where a customer is acting via trustee/nominee/intermediary, reliance includes “**satisfactory evidence**” of:
- identity of “**intermediaries**”
- identity of “**persons on whose behalf they are acting**”
- and “**details of the nature of the trust or other arrangements in place**.” [E. On-going Due Diligence, pp.40-44]

## 5) What is missing to answer “for X” as a concrete case
The provided sections do not describe what “X” is (company/partnership/trust/etc.), whether X (or controlling-interest owner) is listed (which can remove the BO identification/verification requirement), or the ownership/control structure (shareholding %, partner profit share %, trust deed/beneficiary interests, control rights). Without those facts, BO identification can only be stated in the above rule-based form. [C. Definitions, pp.5-6; E. On-going Due Diligence, pp.40-44]
